{"query":"Firecrawl MCP search API agent context official docs","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.firecrawl.dev/blog/firecrawl-mcp-in-cursor","title":"How to Set Up and Use Firecrawl MCP in Cursor","content":"### I see a red dot next to the server name. What do I do?

Click refresh. If that fails, check `npx` path (`which npx`). The most common issue is Cursor not finding your Node.js installation.

### How many MCP servers can I run at once?

Cursor supports ~40 active MCP tools simultaneously.

### Will the agent automatically use Firecrawl?

Yes. If you ask to "search the web" or "scrape this site," the agent automatically selects the Firecrawl tool. You can also be explicit: "Use Firecrawl to..."

placeholder [...] The agent scrapes the page to get a `scrapeId`, then calls firecrawl\_interact with natural language prompts to search, click, and extract — all within the same browser session. You can also pass Playwright code for precise control. Sessions support persistent profiles so you can stay logged in across scrapes.

`scrapeId`

## Step-by-step setup guide

Fortunately, it's extremely easy to set up Firecrawl MCP in Cursor.

### Prerequisites

If you'd rather skip the manual config entirely, Firecrawl is available as an official Cursor plugin — one click in the Cursor Marketplace and you're done, no JSON or Node.js required.

For the MCP server setup, you need three things before starting:

`node --version`

### Step 1: Configure the Firecrawl MCP in Cursor [...] | Tool | Best Use Case |
 --- |
| firecrawl\_scrape | You know the exact URL and want content fast. |
| firecrawl\_search | You need to find information but don't know the URL. |
| firecrawl\_extract | You need specific data fields (price, email) in structured JSON. |
| firecrawl\_map | You want to discover all pages on a site (sitemap). |
| firecrawl\_deep\_research | You need comprehensive analysis across many sources. |
| firecrawl\_agent | You need autonomous browsing/navigation (login, click buttons). |
| firecrawl\_interact | You need to scrape a page then take actions in it (search, click, fill forms). Docs → |
| firecrawl\_batch\_scrape | You have a list of multiple URLs to process in parallel. |

## Start agentic engineering today","score":0.99991906,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://docs.firecrawl.dev/introduction","title":"Introduction | Firecrawl","content":"### ​ Use Firecrawl with AI agents (recommended)

The Firecrawl skill is the fastest way for agents to discover and use Firecrawl. Without it, your agent will not know Firecrawl is available.

```
npx -y firecrawl-cli@latest init --all --browser

```

Restart your agent after installing the skill. See Skill + CLI for the full setup.

Or use the MCP Server to connect Firecrawl directly to Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, and other AI tools.

### ​ Make your first request

Copy the code below, replace `fc-YOUR-API-KEY` with your API key, and run it:

```
curl -X POST ' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer fc-YOUR-API-KEY' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"url": "

```

Example response [...] ### ​ Why Firecrawl?

 Search, scrape, and interact — three APIs that cover to gather web data reliably
 LLM-ready output: Clean markdown, structured JSON, screenshots, and more
 Handles the hard stuff: Proxies, anti-bot, JavaScript rendering, and dynamic content
 Reliable: Built for production with high uptime and consistent results
 Fast: Results in seconds, optimized for high-throughput
 MCP Server: Connect Firecrawl to any AI tool via the Model Context Protocol

## ​ Search

Search the web and get full page content from results in one call. See the Search feature docs for all options.

```
from firecrawl import Firecrawl

firecrawl = Firecrawl(api_key="fc-YOUR-API-KEY")

results = firecrawl.search(
    query="firecrawl",
    limit=3,
)
print(results)

```

# Firecrawl MCP Server

Use Firecrawl’s API through the Model Context Protocol

A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server implementation that integrates Firecrawl for web scraping capabilities. Our MCP server is open-source and available on GitHub.

## ​ Features

 Web scraping, crawling, and discovery
 Search and content extraction
 Deep research with autonomous agent
 Browser session management
 Cloud and self-hosted support
 Streamable HTTP support

## ​ Installation

You can either use our remote hosted URL or run the server locally. Get your API key from 

### ​ Remote hosted URL

Copy

```

```

### ​ Running with npx

Copy

```
env FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=fc-YOUR_API_KEY npx -y firecrawl-mcp env FIRECRAWL_API_KEY=fc-YOUR_API_KEY  npx -y firecrawl-mcp
``` [...] Firecrawl Docs home page

results.title: How to Set Up and Use Firecrawl MCP in Cursor
results.content:
### I see a red dot next to the server name. What do I do?

Click refresh. If that fails, check `npx` path (`which npx`). The most common issue is Cursor not finding your Node.js installation.

### How many MCP servers can I run at once?

Cursor supports ~40 active MCP tools simultaneously.

### Will the agent automatically use Firecrawl?

Yes. If you ask to "search the web" or "scrape this site," the agent automatically selects the Firecrawl tool. You can also be explicit: "Use Firecrawl to..."

placeholder [...] The agent scrapes the page to get a `scrapeId`, then calls firecrawl\_interact with natural language prompts to search, click, and extract — all within the same browser session. You can also pass Playwright code for precise control. Sessions support persistent profiles so you can stay logged in across scrapes.

`scrapeId`

## Step-by-step setup guide

Fortunately, it's extremely easy to set up Firecrawl MCP in Cursor.

### Prerequisites

If you'd rather skip the manual config entirely, Firecrawl is available as an official Cursor plugin — one click in the Cursor Marketplace and you're done, no JSON or Node.js required.

For the MCP server setup, you need three things before starting:

`node --version`

### Step 1: Configure the Firecrawl MCP in Cursor [...] | Tool | Best Use Case |
 --- |
| firecrawl\_scrape | You know the exact URL and want content fast. |
| firecrawl\_search | You need to find information but don't know the URL. |
| firecrawl\_extract | You need specific data fields (price, email) in structured JSON. |
| firecrawl\_map | You want to discover all pages on a site (sitemap). |
| firecrawl\_deep\_research | You need comprehensive analysis across many sources. |
| firecrawl\_agent | You need autonomous browsing/navigation (login, click buttons). |
| firecrawl\_interact | You need to scrape a page then take actions in it (search, click, fill forms). Docs → |
| firecrawl\_batch\_scrape | You have a list of multiple URLs to process in parallel. |

## Start agentic engineering today

results.title: Introduction | Firecrawl
results.content:
### ​ Use Firecrawl with AI agents (recommended)

The Firecrawl skill is the fastest way for agents to discover and use Firecrawl. Without it, your agent will not know Firecrawl is available.

```
npx -y firecrawl-cli@latest init --all --browser

```

Restart your agent after installing the skill. See Skill + CLI for the full setup.

Or use the MCP Server to connect Firecrawl directly to Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, and other AI tools.

### ​ Make your first request

Copy the code below, replace `fc-YOUR-API-KEY` with your API key, and run it:

```
curl -X POST ' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer fc-YOUR-API-KEY' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"url": "

```

Example response [...] ### ​ Why Firecrawl?

 Search, scrape, and interact — three APIs that cover to gather web data reliably
 LLM-ready output: Clean markdown, structured JSON, screenshots, and more
 Handles the hard stuff: Proxies, anti-bot, JavaScript rendering, and dynamic content
 Reliable: Built for production with high uptime and consistent results
 Fast: Results in seconds, optimized for high-throughput
 MCP Server: Connect Firecrawl to any AI tool via the Model Context Protocol

## ​ Search

Search the web and get full page content from results in one call. See the Search feature docs for all options.

```
from firecrawl import Firecrawl

firecrawl = Firecrawl(api_key="fc-YOUR-API-KEY")

results = firecrawl.search(
    query="firecrawl",
    limit=3,
)
print(results)

```

Response [...] Skip to main content

results.content:
#### Agent builders

Build agents that can search, scrape, and extract structured data from the web on demand.

#### Internal developer tooling

Standardize one MCP integration for teams so everyone uses the same reliable web data layer.

#### From prototypes to production

Start on the free tier, then scale without adding headless browsers, proxies, or scraper maintenance.

Claude Code
Cursor
Windsurf

### How it works

#### Install the Firecrawl MCP server

Run one command (`npx -y firecrawl-mcp`) to install the official server.

#### Connect it to your editor

Add your API key once and use it in Cursor, Claude Code, Windsurf, and more.

#### Find the right pages, fast

Let your agent use Search to discover sources, then Scrape to pull fresh context.

#### Extract LLM-ready data [...] Learn to build custom MCP servers in Python using FastMCP. Step-by-step tutorial covering tools, resources, prompts, debugging, and deployment with Firecrawl integration.

15 Best MCP Servers for AI Agents (Including Firecrawl)

results.title: API for AI Agents: Types, Integration Patterns, and Tools
results.content:
APIs fill in that knowledge gap. Start with Firecrawl for web context — it’s the fastest way to give your agent access to real-time information from the web. Layer in MCP servers or a unified API platform for SaaS integrations, and direct function calling for the internal APIs you control.

One thing worth noting as you build: through 2025, MCP servers were considered the gold standard for giving agents access to external tools. In 2026, that thinking has shifted. CLIs are now considered better for agents — they’re faster to integrate, easier to debug, and give agents more direct, predictable access to the tools they need. If you’re starting a new agent project today, CLIs are worth defaulting to before reaching for an MCP server. [...] A specialized scraping API handles all of this and returns clean, LLM-ready content instead of raw HTML full of noise. Firecrawl covers the full stack:

Firecrawl is the best web data API for AI agents today — the numbers say it all: 100k+ GitHub stars, nearly a million users, and a top 100 GitHub repository. For any agent that does research, monitors competitors, pulls documentation, or needs current information from the web, this is the first integration to add.

You can also use the Firecrawl CLI directly with your agents for better programmatic control and operational efficiency.

### 2. CRM and sales APIs

results.title: How to Set Up and Use Firecrawl MCP in Cursor
results.content:
### I see a red dot next to the server name. What do I do?

Click refresh. If that fails, check `npx` path (`which npx`). The most common issue is Cursor not finding your Node.js installation.

### How many MCP servers can I run at once?

Cursor supports ~40 active MCP tools simultaneously.

### Will the agent automatically use Firecrawl?

Yes. If you ask to "search the web" or "scrape this site," the agent automatically selects the Firecrawl tool. You can also be explicit: "Use Firecrawl to..."

placeholder [...] The agent scrapes the page to get a `scrapeId`, then calls firecrawl\_interact with natural language prompts to search, click, and extract — all within the same browser session. You can also pass Playwright code for precise control. Sessions support persistent profiles so you can stay logged in across scrapes.

`scrapeId`

## Step-by-step setup guide

Fortunately, it's extremely easy to set up Firecrawl MCP in Cursor.

### Prerequisites

If you'd rather skip the manual config entirely, Firecrawl is available as an official Cursor plugin — one click in the Cursor Marketplace and you're done, no JSON or Node.js required.

For the MCP server setup, you need three things before starting:

`node --version`

### Step 1: Configure the Firecrawl MCP in Cursor [...] | Tool | Best Use Case |
 --- |
| firecrawl\_scrape | You know the exact URL and want content fast. |
| firecrawl\_search | You need to find information but don't know the URL. |
| firecrawl\_extract | You need specific data fields (price, email) in structured JSON. |
| firecrawl\_map | You want to discover all pages on a site (sitemap). |
| firecrawl\_deep\_research | You need comprehensive analysis across many sources. |
| firecrawl\_agent | You need autonomous browsing/navigation (login, click buttons). |
| firecrawl\_interact | You need to scrape a page then take actions in it (search, click, fill forms). Docs → |
| firecrawl\_batch\_scrape | You have a list of multiple URLs to process in parallel. |

## Start agentic engineering today

results.title: Introduction | Firecrawl
results.content:
### ​ Use Firecrawl with AI agents (recommended)

The Firecrawl skill is the fastest way for agents to discover and use Firecrawl. Without it, your agent will not know Firecrawl is available.

```
npx -y firecrawl-cli@latest init --all --browser

```

Restart your agent after installing the skill. See Skill + CLI for the full setup.

Or use the MCP Server to connect Firecrawl directly to Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, VS Code, and other AI tools.

### ​ Make your first request

Copy the code below, replace `fc-YOUR-API-KEY` with your API key, and run it:

```
curl -X POST ' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer fc-YOUR-API-KEY' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"url": "

```

Example response [...] ### ​ Why Firecrawl?

 Search, scrape, and interact — three APIs that cover to gather web data reliably
 LLM-ready output: Clean markdown, structured JSON, screenshots, and more
 Handles the hard stuff: Proxies, anti-bot, JavaScript rendering, and dynamic content
 Reliable: Built for production with high uptime and consistent results
 Fast: Results in seconds, optimized for high-throughput
 MCP Server: Connect Firecrawl to any AI tool via the Model Context Protocol

## ​ Search

Search the web and get full page content from results in one call. See the Search feature docs for all options.

```
from firecrawl import Firecrawl

firecrawl = Firecrawl(api_key="fc-YOUR-API-KEY")

results = firecrawl.search(
    query="firecrawl",
    limit=3,
)
print(results)

```

results.title: API for AI Agents: Types, Integration Patterns, and Tools
results.content:
APIs fill in that knowledge gap. Start with Firecrawl for web context — it’s the fastest way to give your agent access to real-time information from the web. Layer in MCP servers or a unified API platform for SaaS integrations, and direct function calling for the internal APIs you control.

One thing worth noting as you build: through 2025, MCP servers were considered the gold standard for giving agents access to external tools. In 2026, that thinking has shifted. CLIs are now considered better for agents — they’re faster to integrate, easier to debug, and give agents more direct, predictable access to the tools they need. If you’re starting a new agent project today, CLIs are worth defaulting to before reaching for an MCP server. [...] A specialized scraping API handles all of this and returns clean, LLM-ready content instead of raw HTML full of noise. Firecrawl covers the full stack:

Firecrawl is the best web data API for AI agents today — the numbers say it all: 100k+ GitHub stars, nearly a million users, and a top 100 GitHub repository. For any agent that does research, monitors competitors, pulls documentation, or needs current information from the web, this is the first integration to add.

You can also use the Firecrawl CLI directly with your agents for better programmatic control and operational efficiency.
